column1: # mapping_set_title: "Grounding of PYRAMID PRA component entities in IES/HQDM (worked bridge example)"

# comment: "Candidate-for-review grounding of the subject-matter-semantics entities of two PYRAMID Reference Architecture (PRA) components (Geography, 5.4.2.23; Tactical Objects, 5.4.2.60) into IES/HQDM terms, using the crosswalk in this repository. Source: PYRAMID Technical Standard V1.0 (Def Stan 00-134), OGL v3.0. The pyrgeo:/pyrtac: identifiers are minted by Tesseract to reference PRA entities that have no public IRIs; they are NOT official MOD identifiers. Purpose: give a PYRAMID inter-component bridge (Technical Standard s5.2, which performs 'data element mapping ... to bridge the semantic gap') a consistent, published semantic reference. See README.md."

column8: An entity in the battlespace ... with associated characteristics (allegiance, classification, location and kinematics) (PYD TS 5.4.2.60.5.1). A whole-life persisting thing = ies:Entity. Via the crosswalk ies:Entity ~ hqdm:individual, with hqdm:physical_object the narrower option for tangible objects. This is the IES (operational-picture) side of the bridge.

The estimated pattern of action ... and operational state of a Tactical_Object (e.g. ready for operation, operational in air). A condition holding over a temporal part of the object = ies:State (crosswalk: ies:State ~ hqdm:state).

NON-ATOMIC. One entity conflates mereology ("equipment on a vehicle, or vehicles within a formation" = ies:isPartOf ~ hqdm:part_of) with associative happenings ("a missile targeting an aircraft" = an ies:Event with participants). A faithful bridge must split this in EDOAL, not map it 1:1. See README divergence D3.

A source of information about objects of tactical significance: a device or organisation providing observations, i.e. an ies:Entity in a reporting role. Loose; the role, not the type, is what matters.

A specific type of Object_Interest_Requirement (e.g. a requirement to determine the type of Tactical_Object). A powertype term over tactical objects; aligns with the ies:ClassOf... second-order hierarchy (crosswalk: ies:ClassOfElement ~ hqdm:class_of_spatio_temporal_extent).

FALSE FRIEND across components. Here Capability = "the capability to perform a range of services, including maintaining knowledge of Tactical_Objects". A disposition with no clean 4D-ontology counterpart. Recorded ONLY to warn: do NOT unify pyrtac:Capability with pyrgeo:Capability on the shared label. See README divergence D2.

A geographical feature of the Earth ... e.g. a specific river, the land border between two countries (PYD TS 5.4.2.23.5.1); tangible (buildings) and intangible (country border); points, areas or volumes. Tangible features = hqdm:physical_object; this is the HQDM / built-and-physical-environment side of the bridge. Intangible features (a border) are spatial abstractions and map less cleanly.

The IES-side grounding of the same entity: a persisting spatial thing is an ies:Entity. Recorded alongside the hqdm:physical_object grounding because the crosswalk (ies:Entity ~ hqdm:individual) is exactly what lets a Geographical_Feature and a Tactical_Object resolve to one referent. See bridge.ttl.
A specific type of Geographical_Feature, e.g. a building, terrain, country border. A powertype term; aligns with ies:ClassOfElement ~ hqdm:class_of_spatio_temporal_extent (crosswalk).
NON-ATOMIC. "e.g. ownship or projected route". Ownship is an ies:Entity; a projected route is a plan/trajectory with no clean IES class. Split required; ownship side grounds cleanly.

The relationship between Geographical_Features, such as the distance between two buildings. Chiefly quantitative spatial relations (distance, bearing), which are NOT mereological. Grounded only loosely; a bridge should express these as measured spatial relations, not part-of. Low confidence by design.

FALSE FRIEND across components. Here Capability = "the ability to provide information on a particular region or Feature_Type". Different scope from pyrtac:Capability despite the identical label. Do NOT unify the two. See README divergence D2.

The characterisation of a real-world entity, based on Evidence from one or more sources (PYD TS 5.4.2.14.5.1). The Data Fusion component produces Fused_Objects that become Tactical_Objects downstream; grounding both in ies:Entity makes the Sensing -> Data Fusion -> Tactical Objects -> Geography chain share one referent type. Shows the grounding generalises beyond a single pair.
The type of real-world entity (e.g. vehicle type or weapon type). A powertype term over fused objects; aligns with the ies:ClassOf... hierarchy.

THIRD member of the Capability false-friend family (with pyrgeo:Capability and pyrtac:Capability). "The range of Evidence_Types that can be processed and ... Fused_Object_Types that can be generated". Same label root, component-specific scope. Do NOT unify across components. The false friend is a pattern, not a one-off. See README D2.
